evidence_type: functional-test report (harness end-to-end run log, both CLI paths)
revision_note: HEAD at execution; the harness is an UNCOMMITTED working-tree addition on top of this SHA (the wave conductor owns commits). Working-tree delta for this task: internal/cli/e2e_scaffold_harness_test.go (new).
execution_environment: macOS Darwin 25.5.0 arm64, local dev workstation; isolated t.TempDir() product dirs; fully offline (file:// module proxies, GOSUMDB=off, GOPRIVATE/GONOPROXY neutralized)
result: PASS — all five pipeline steps (init, go mod tidy, go mod download, go build ./..., boot smoke test) succeeded end to end for BOTH CLI paths
harness_design: scaffoldPipeline(t, cli, modulePath, initArgs, goEnv) drives a REAL wowapi binary (subprocess, not in-process) through init -> go mod tidy -> go mod download -> go build ./... -> a written-in boot-and-validate smoke test (kernel.New -> app.Register(wire.Modules()) -> app.Boot, no DB via a no-op TxManager). Every step failure reports its step name plus FULL command output (task-002 detailed-work step 5). The two CLI paths are two binaries built from this checkout: `go build -buildvcs=false` (source/devel) and the same plus `-ldflags -X ...buildinfo.version=v0.1.0` (released stand-in — the release pipeline's own stamping mechanism). The released path resolves github.com/qatoolist/wowapi@v0.1.0 from a local file:// GOPROXY packaged from this checkout (list/.info/.mod/.zip), chained with the local module cache's download dir for dependencies — hermetic, yet exercising the same proxy-resolution path a published release does. DX-02's T004 already consumes the same underlying scaffold primitive (buildRenderedProduct, itself now built on `init --local-framework`); a future DX-04 story calls scaffoldPipeline with different generator args/assertions.

runs.outcome: Pre-fix, the pipeline's `go mod download` step failed on a pristine devel-CLI scaffold ('unknown revision v0.0.0') while init itself reported success — captured in t1-t4-prefix-failfirst.log (shared with EV-001; the same defect is what breaks the pipeline). Post-fix, TestE2EScaffoldSourceBuiltCLI permanently asserts this failure mode is impossible: a flag-less devel init fails closed pre-write (exit != 0, zero files, remediation present) before the pipeline would even start.

TestE2EScaffoldSourceBuiltCLI: devel binary, fail-closed guard PASS, then init --local-framework -> full pipeline PASS (5.39s). TestE2EScaffoldReleasedCLI: v0.1.0-stamped binary, flag-less init pins v0.1.0 (no replace directive), framework fetched from the file proxy -> full pipeline PASS (6.60s). Each of the five steps individually logged ok in both runs.
go test ./internal/cli/ -count=1 -> ok (24.4s), including every pre-existing scaffold/gen/i18n/config test now flowing through the new resolution paths; go test ./internal/buildinfo/ -> ok.

flakiness_mitigation: No network is touched on any step: both proxies are file:// URLs (the packaged-checkout proxy and the local module cache), GOSUMDB=off, GOWORK=off, and developer-machine GOPRIVATE/GONOPROXY overrides are explicitly neutralized in the harness env — removing the go-mod-download flakiness risk task-002's risk section flags.
